title: Home blood pressure log review

red_flags.text: Lists severe blood pressure, confusion, visual changes, chest pain, breathlessness, neurological symptoms, severe headache, and specialist referral criteria.
red_flags.rationale: The exact DHA red-flag section supports clinician-assessed severe readings, confusion, visual change, chest pain, breathlessness, neurological symptoms, severe headache, and escalation concern without asserting absence.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists severe blood pressure, confusion, visual changes, chest pain, breathlessness, neurological symptoms, severe headache, and specialist referral criteria.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Hypertension – 27

medication.text: Supports clinic, home, or ambulatory blood-pressure monitoring, symptoms, medication and lifestyle discussion, and an annual review without generating targets, treatment, or advice.
medication.rationale: The exact monitoring section supports clinician-entered training discussion, target context, questions, plan, and arranged follow-up without generating a target or treatment change.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports clinic, home, or ambulatory blood-pressure monitoring, symptoms, medication and lifestyle discussion, and an annual review without generating targets, treatment, or advice.

source.title: Hypertension in adults: diagnosis and management

follow_up.text: Supports documenting repeated clinic readings, ABPM or HBPM source and averages, result context, annual measurement in relevant diabetes care, and clinician review without automatically diagnosing hypertension.
follow_up.rationale: The exact HBPM section supports recording repeated home readings, measurement source, schedule, averages, and clinic comparison without automatically diagnosing hypertension.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports documenting repeated clinic readings, ABPM or HBPM source and averages, result context, annual measurement in relevant diabetes care, and clinician review without automatically diagnosing hypertension.

source.title: Hypertension in adults: diagnosis and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1-1.2.13 - clinic, ambulatory, and home blood-pressure assessment

follow_up.text: Supports validated and maintained devices, standardized measurement conditions, appropriate cuff size, pulse review, and clinician-recorded postural measurements.
follow_up.rationale: The exact technique section supports clinician review of device validation, maintenance, environment, seated posture, supported arm, cuff size, pulse, and measured observations without inferring correct technique.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports validated and maintained devices, standardized measurement conditions, appropriate cuff size, pulse review, and clinician-recorded postural measurements.

source_limitations: Device, technique, dates, readings, averages, symptoms, pregnancy, diagnosis, target, medicine, treatment response, escalation, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
